page.content: In the rapidly evolving landscape of the Asia-Pacific hedge fund sector, the analysis of expense ratios and strategy returns is critical for assessing performance and competitiveness. The data presented in this report focuses on the expense ratio benchmarking of leading hedge funds, including GIC Private Limited.

lines.text: In the rapidly evolving landscape of the Asia-Pacific hedge fund sector, the analysis of expense ratios and strategy returns is
critical for assessing performance and competitiveness. The data presented in this report focuses on the expense ratio
benchmarking of leading hedge funds, including GIC Private Limited.

page.content: Notably, GIC Private Limited's expense ratio in 2020 was reported at 0.45%, a figure that exemplifies its commitment to maintaining operational efficiency while delivering value to investors.

lines.text: Notably, GIC Private Limited's expense ratio in 2020 was reported at 0.45%, a figure that exemplifies its commitment to
maintaining operational efficiency while delivering value to investors.

page.content: As we examine the annual returns across various strategy types, it is evident that the Market Neutral strategy, with an assets under management of ¥250 billion, recorded an annual return of 6.5%. In contrast, the Event Driven strategy, which managed ¥180 billion, achieved a more robust annual return of 10.2%. This table further illustrates trends over the years, with the industry average expense ratio for 2020 standing at 0.52%, while the ratio dropped to 0.37% in 2021, indicating a broader shift towards cost-effective management practices within the industry. Collectively, these metrics provide a comprehensive overview of how expense management and strategic positioning are instrumental in enhancing overall fund performance in a competitive market.
